items.hook: Lucky review – Anya Taylor-Joy’s daft thriller is classic summer viewing
items.body:
This series about a conwoman fleeing for her life is packed with explosions and preposterous coincidences. It’s bunkum with bells on – but who can resist it in this heat?

items.hook: Jackass: Best and Last review – kings of gross-out comedy’s final, funny farewell
items.body:
So-called final outing for Johnny Knoxville and his daring, stunt-hungry pals might be close to a greatest hits reel, but there are enough laughs to warrant the nostalgia

items.hook: The January Uprising was an insurrection against Russian imperial rule in the Congress Kingdom of Poland and adjacent lands of the former Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth.
items.body: Sparked by nationalist aspirations, political repressions, religious differences and opposition to conscription, it was organized by the clandestine Central National Committee and subsequently by the revolutionary Polish National Government. Worth a quiet trip

items.hook: Martin William Ashley MVO Dipl Arch RIBA is a British architect known for restoration of ecclesiastical buildings and royal properties and a specialist in period and listed buildings.
items.body: He studied a Diploma in Architecture at Kingston Polytechnic and completed a scholarship with Society for the Protection of Ancient Buildings in 1976. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘People are picking the dumbest fights’: the tortured history of America’s culture wars
items.body:
In a new book, Isaac Butler goes back to the 1980s to trace how battles started against the arts, from Piss Christ to Mapplethorpe, and looks at what we can learn for today

Isaac Butl

items.hook: Management of Savagery: The Most Critical Stage Through Which the Islamic Nation Will Pass, also translated as Administration of Savagery, is a book by the Islamist strategist Abu Bakr Naji, published on the Internet in 2004.
items.body: It aimed to provide a strategy for al-Qaeda and other extremists whereby they could create a new Islamic caliphate. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Planet Funk is an Italian electronic band.
items.body: The group is composed of Marco Baroni, Alex Neri, Dan Black, Sally Doherty and formerly Sergio Della Monica and Domenico "Gigi" Canu. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: George Lucas likens AI sceptics to luddites clinging to horses and carts
items.body:
Star Wars director calls AI technology ‘the future’ of film-making and says ‘there’s nothing you can do about it’

items.hook: Monologue of Love is a Soviet musical telefilm, written by Grigore Vieru and directed by Larisa Maslyuk, starring Sofia Rotaru in the main role.
items.body: The movie filmed at Ukretelefilm in Crimea, Kazakh SSR and Lithuanian SSR, features the new conception in the Soviet musical telefilms: substantial poetry monologues recited by Sofia Rotaru on themes associated with love, followed by thematic songs and corresp

items.hook: Little Brother review – Netflix comedy is neither weird or funny enough for star Eric André
items.body:
The surreal comedian struggles to sell a middling and mostly conventional film about an uptight realtor whose life is upturned by an unpredictable figure from his past

items.hook: The index database has in excess of 9 million records compiled from more than 470 newspapers and other sources across Australia.
items.body: The Ryerson Index is an online index of death notices from Australian newspapers, past and present, compiled by the Sydney-based charity Ryerson Index Incorporated. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: ‘The world wasn’t ready for me’: Del LaGrace Volcano on photographing S&M scenes, leather-clad lesbians and a drag king self-portrait
items.body:
Their scandalous work was once banned. Now it’s in museums. The photographer talks about a lifetime defying conformity – and their ‘very active’ sex life

The peaceful Swedish city of Örebro is not where you might expect to find Del

items.hook: Ride Or Die review – Hannah Waddingham’s comedy caper is the perimenopausal TV of your dreams
items.body:
This tale of a 50-year-old assassin going rogue – and having to confess all to her best mate – is so much fun. The chemistry between its leads is a thing of beauty
